_description: Monthly water temperature normals (°F) for Arkansas reservoirs and lakes.
_usage: Array index 0 = January … 11 = December. Used as Tier 3 fallback when no live sensor data is available. Values derived from USGS historical records, published lake studies, and observed seasonal patterns.
_accuracy: ±4–6°F. Actual temperature varies with annual precipitation, air temperature, and reservoir stratification. Values represent long-term mid-month surface averages.

_sources.NW_Ozark_reservoirs: USGS Arkansas Water Science Center historical parameter 00010 data; White River reservoir thermal profiles. Deep, clear-water Ozark impoundments warm slowly — winter readings remain colder longer than south AR lakes.
_sources.Ouachita_reservoirs: USGS Lower Mississippi-Gulf Water Science Center; Ouachita National Forest water quality studies. Slightly warmer than NW AR due to lower elevation and more solar exposure.
_sources.Coastal_Plain_lakes: AGFC weekly fishing reports (multi-year); USGS gauges on Ouachita and Red Rivers. Shallowest and warmest category.
_sources.Central_AR_urban: USGS 07263300 (Lake Maumelle) historical 00010 readings; observed reports from CAW and AGFC.
